mission.title: Install and Configure Easegress on Kubernetes
mission.description: Easegress is a Cloud Native traffic orchestration system that provides high availability, traffic management, and observability features. Installing Easegress allows you to efficiently manage and route traffic in your Kubernetes environment.

steps.title: Add the Easegress Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official Easegress Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration with the following command:
```bash
helm repo add megaease https://megaease.github.io/easegress-charts
```

steps.title: Install Easegress using Helm
steps.description:
Install Easegress in a new namespace called 'easegress' with the following command:
```bash
helm install easegress megaease/easegress --namespace easegress --create-namespace --version v2.10.1
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Easegress pods are running successfully by executing:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace easegress
```

steps.title: Expose the Easegress service
steps.description:
To access the Easegress service, you can port-forward it to your local machine:
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/easegress --namespace easegress 8080:80
```

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
You may want to configure resource limits for the Easegress deployment. Edit the deployment with:
```bash
kubectl edit deployment easegress --namespace easegress
``` 
Add resource limits under the containers section:
```yaml
resources:
  limits:
    cpu: "500m"
    memory: "512Mi"
  requests:
    cpu: "250m"
    memory: "256Mi"
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Easegress will show the pods running in the 'easegress' namespace when you execute 'kubectl get pods'. You should also be able to access the service via port-forwarding on localhost:8080.
codeSnippets: helm install easegress megaease/easegress --namespace easegress --create-namespace --version v2.10.1

prerequisites.description: You need a Kubernetes cluster and Helm installed to deploy Easegress.
